title: Add image size option for blog post images

requirements.summary: Add image size selector controls to blog post forms and apply selected sizes in the detail view
acceptanceCriteria: An image size selector (dropdown or radio group) appears alongside each image upload field in CreateBlogPostPage and EditBlogPostPage.
Predefined size options are available: Small (25%), Medium (50%), Large (75%), Full Width (100%).
A custom option allows the user to enter explicit pixel or percentage width/height values.
The chosen image size is persisted with the post data (stored as image metadata or CSS class reference).
In BlogPostDetailPage, images are rendered with the width/height corresponding to the selected size option.
Existing posts without a size setting default to Full Width to preserve backward compatibility.

file_operations.description: Add an image size selector control (dropdown with options: Small 25%, Medium 50%, Large 75%, Full Width 100%, Custom) alongside the featured image upload field. Add state to track the selected size and custom width/height values. Pass the selected imageSize value to the createPost mutation as a string (e.g., 'small', 'medium', 'large', 'full', or 'custom:WxH'). Include form validation to ensure custom dimensions are provided when Custom is selected.

Add an image size selector control (dropdown with options: Small 25%, Medium 50%, Large 75%, Full Width 100%, Custom) alongside the featured image upload field. Pre-populate the selector with the existing post's imageSize value if present, defaulting to Full Width if null. Add state to track the selected size and custom width/height values. Pass the updated imageSize value to the updatePost mutation. Include form validation to ensure custom dimensions are provided when Custom is selected.

Modify the featured image and content images rendering logic to apply inline width styles based on the post's imageSize field. Map 'small' to 25% width, 'medium' to 50%, 'large' to 75%, 'full' or null/undefined to 100%. For 'custom:WxH' format, parse and apply the specified dimensions. Ensure images maintain aspect ratio and are responsive (max-width: 100%). Default to Full Width (100%) for backward compatibility when imageSize is absent.
